title: Passive voice in news reporting
rule: Events are often reported with passive verbs (was held, were injured) to focus on what happened rather than who did it.
example: A memorial service was held Sunday.
meaning: ニュースでは受動態を使い、出来事そのものに焦点を当てる。誰が行ったかより「何が起きたか」を前面に出す書き方。
note: Memorial service marks 25 years since fatal stabbing rampage

title: With + noun + -ing (absolute construction)
rule: With + noun + present participle adds background detail about simultaneous actions or circumstances.
example: with about 760 bereaved family members praying for the souls of the victims
meaning: 「～が…しながら／～という状況で」という付帯状況を簡潔に添える構文。主節の動作と同時に起きた背景を説明する。
note: Memorial service marks 25 years since fatal stabbing rampage

title: Defining and non-defining relative clauses
rule: That/which/who clauses add essential or extra information about a noun; preposition + which is formal and common in news.
example: on which the names of the eight children who died are engraved
meaning: 関係代名詞節で名詞を詳しく説明する。前置詞＋which はフォーマルなニュース文体でよく使われる。
note: Memorial service marks 25 years since fatal stabbing rampage

title: Present participle for result (prompting ...)
rule: A present participle phrase after a main clause can show a result or consequence of the main action.
example: The incident shook the school's "myth of safety," prompting the strengthening of safety measures.
meaning: 主節の後に現在分詞句を置き、その結果・付随的な影響を述べる。because 節より簡潔で、ニュース記事でよく見る。
note: Memorial service marks 25 years since fatal stabbing rampage

title: Participial phrases modifying nouns
rule: Past/present participles and participial phrases often follow nouns to add detail (e.g., dating back to..., believed to have been built..., long cherished by...).
example: Castles with wooden structures dating back to the feudal era were destroyed.
meaning: 名詞の後ろに分詞句を置き、時代・由来・評価などの情報をコンパクトに足す。ニュース記事で人物・建造物を説明する際に頻出。
note: Deteriorating castles in Japan face high costs, debates over renovation

title: Passive voice in news reporting
rule: Passive forms (was destroyed, has been closed, was identified) focus on events and outcomes rather than who performed the action.
example: The original keep was destroyed in the 1945 U.S. atomic bombing.
meaning: 受動態で事実・結果を客観的に伝える。誰が行ったかより「何が起きたか」を前面に出す報道文体の特徴。
note: Deteriorating castles in Japan face high costs, debates over renovation

title: Contrast with Although / While / But
rule: Although/While introduce contrast or concession; But links contrasting main clauses. Together they structure problem–response narratives.
example: Although they were later rebuilt, they have deteriorated over time.
meaning: Although/While は譲歩（〜だが）、But は逆接。再建という努力と劣化という現実を対比し、記事の問題提起を作る。
note: Deteriorating castles in Japan face high costs, debates over renovation

title: Reporting verbs with that-clauses
rule: Verbs like said, argued, added, identified are followed by that-clauses (or direct quotes) to report speech, findings, or official positions.
example: A city official said the castle tower is a symbol of the city.
meaning: said, argued, added などの報告動詞＋that節（または直接引用）で、担当者・専門家・調査結果の見解を伝える。
note: Deteriorating castles in Japan face high costs, debates over renovation

title: thanks to + noun phrase
rule: Use 'thanks to' to show a positive cause or contributing factor.
example: Japan posted a surplus, thanks to returns from robust overseas investments.
meaning: thanks to は「〜のおかげで」と原因・要因を示す。because of より成果への寄与がポジティブなニュアンスになりやすい。
note: Japan's April current account surplus at ¥3.91 tril on overseas returns

title: Reported speech with end placement
rule: In news writing, the source (e.g., 'the ministry said') often comes at the end of the sentence.
example: The surplus surged 64.9 percent, the Finance Ministry said.
meaning: 報道英語では発言元を文末に置く倒置が頻出。日本語訳では文頭に「〜によると」と訳すと自然。
note: Japan's April current account surplus at ¥3.91 tril on overseas returns

title: Non-defining relative clauses (which ...)
rule: Use commas + 'which' to add extra information about a noun without restricting its meaning.
example: Primary income, which reflects how much Japan earned, rose 15.3 percent.
meaning: カンマで挟んだ which 節は補足説明。訳すときは「〜という、」や括弧的に挿入すると読みやすい。
note: Japan's April current account surplus at ¥3.91 tril on overseas returns

title: Participial phrases for cause/background
rule: Present participles (-ing) and past participles can add background or cause in concise news style.
example: The conflict, stemming from the launch of attacks on Iran, impacted the figure.
meaning: stemming from や shedding など分詞構文で、理由や付加情報をコンパクトに挿入するのがニュース文体の特徴。
note: Japan's April current account surplus at ¥3.91 tril on overseas returns

title: Reported speech in news (said that ...)
rule: Use 'said/reported/stated that + clause' to report what an organization or person announced. Tense may shift back (backshift) in formal reporting.
example: The hospitals said that information was confirmed to be on hard disks sold online.
meaning: 報道では said that ... で発表内容を間接的に伝える。that 以下の時制は、発言時より前の事実なら過去形にずらす（時制の一致）ことが多い。
note: Data on 510,000 people linked to Hokkaido hospitals possibly leaked online

title: Passive voice for events and confirmations
rule: News writing often uses passive forms (was confirmed, were destroyed, were recovered) to focus on what happened rather than who did it.
example: Information was confirmed to be on hard disks sold online.
meaning: ニュースでは受動態で「何が確認されたか・何が回収されたか」を主語に置き、行為者より事象を前面に出す。
note: Data on 510,000 people linked to Hokkaido hospitals possibly leaked online

title: Past participles as reduced relative clauses
rule: Past participles (sold, recovered, based) after nouns shorten relative clauses: 'disks (that were) sold online'.
example: The Sapporo-based hospitals recovered 90 similar hard disks.
meaning: 過去分詞を名詞の後に置くと関係代名詞＋be動詞を省略した形になる。Sapporo-based = based in Sapporo、sold online = that were sold online。
note: Data on 510,000 people linked to Hokkaido hospitals possibly leaked online

title: Of + total, number + part (partial amounts)
rule: Pattern 'Of the [total number] ..., [smaller number] ...' shows how many belong to a larger group.
example: Of the 90 similar hard disks recovered, 33 contained information.
meaning: Of the 90 ... , 33 ... は「90のうち33」という部分と全体の関係を示す。日本語の「〜のうち」に相当する。
note: Data on 510,000 people linked to Hokkaido hospitals possibly leaked online

title: on suspicion of + noun
rule: Used in crime news to mean someone was arrested or investigated because they are believed to have committed an offense.
example: Police arrested a woman on suspicion of child abandonment.
meaning: 犯罪報道でよく使われる定型表現。「〜の疑いで」という意味。動詞は arrest, detain, question などと結びつく。
note: Child comes into store saying she's hungry after being left alone for 11 hours by mother, boyfriend who were out drinking

title: be suspected of + -ing
rule: The passive pattern reports an allegation without stating it as fact.
example: The two are suspected of leaving the daughter alone.
meaning: 「〜した疑いがある」と疑いを伝える受動的な言い方。事実断定を避けるニュース文体で頻出。
note: Child comes into store saying she's hungry after being left alone for 11 hours by mother, boyfriend who were out drinking

title: Reporting clause at the end
rule: News articles often place the source (e.g., a broadcaster or police) after the main information.
example: ..., Hokkaido Broadcasting Corp reported.
meaning: 報道文では主な情報を先に述べ、文末に「〜が報じた」と情報源を置く倒置が多い。
note: Child comes into store saying she's hungry after being left alone for 11 hours by mother, boyfriend who were out drinking

title: Participial clause with -ing
rule: An -ing clause can add extra detail about what someone said or did at the same time.
example: An employee reported that a girl had come in alone, saying she was hungry.
meaning: 分詞構文 saying ... で「〜と言って」という付加情報を簡潔に付ける。間接話法と組み合わせると報道文が読みやすくなる。
note: Child comes into store saying she's hungry after being left alone for 11 hours by mother, boyfriend who were out drinking
